text:
was the younger sister of Grisha Jaeger and as an Eldian, she grew up in the Liberio internment zone in Marley.

Appearance

Fay had short, brown hair that barely reached her shoulders with messy bangs and dark eyes. She wore a flowing dress, a white scarf, and a coat, along with an armband which identified her as an Eldian citizen.

Personality

Fay did not have much experience with the world at such a young age, needing Grisha to push her out of her comfort zones. She was an innocent child but was often reliant on her older brother for protection. She saw the world with whimsy and delight, looking at the airship in the sky in awe.

History

One day, after leaving their home, Fay and her brother Grisha caught sight of an airship, which fascinated them. When the airship flew over their city limits wall, they followed it to the higher end of town, leaving their town without permission. While searching for the airship, Fay and Grisha were harassed by the Marleyan citizens who commented on their armbands and referred to them as "rats" and "demon spawn"- indicating the widely-held Marleyan belief that Eldians are impure and undesirable. After reaching a ridge overlooking the Blimp Hangar, Fay and Grisha encountered two Marley officers slacking off nearby. Because Grisha and Fay had left the internment zone without an exit permit, they were offered a choice between enforced labor, or corporal punishment. Grisha requested the physical punishment, in a bid to prevent his parents being involved, and claimed to have brought Fay against her will, offering to take the burden for both of them. One of the men beat up Grisha while the other took Fay away. However, Fay was not present when Grisha returned home after his beating.

The next day, Fay's mutilated body was found by the riverbank. The officers who encountered the children met with their parents and told them that Fay had been safely left at the border, that the two officers' prohibitive workload prevented them escorting her all the way home, and that her death was Fay's own fault for having left the internment zone unaccompanied in the first place. Grisha, being aware that the men had been slacking off and dozing in the sunshine, was certain that the men were lying, and that Fay's death could not be her own fault. While their mother was overcome with sorrow, their father shamelessly demeaned himself to the officers, obsequiously assuring them that he would better educate his son in the sins of his ancestors, and the importance of not leaving the internment zone. Later, Jaeger re-educated Grisha on their ancestors' evil past. When Grisha complained that the Marleyan officers were lying about Fay; that she was blameless, and that he believed that they had had her killed; his father berated him and insisted that all Eldians carry the blame of their ancestors' actions.

Seven years later, Grisha joined the Restorationists after he received information which revealed that Fay was taken by the Marleyan officer who escorted her away that day and was eaten alive by dogs.

Sometime before reporting his parents to the authorities, her nephew wondered if the activities of the Restorationists would lead them to the same fate as her. When Zeke was yelled at by Grisha, he responded with that her death was caused by his decision to see the airship, stunning him.

Fay's death was later avenged as Eren Kruger fifteen years later, who was really an undercover member of the Eldian Restorationists, pushed his fellow officer, Gross, off the borderline wall of Paradis Island, resulting in Gross getting slowly devoured by a Pure Titan.

Relationships

Grisha Jaeger - Grisha was Fay's older brother. As children, the two shared a close, loving relationship. Fay tended to hide behind him when she was afraid, displaying the trust Fay had for her brother. She cared for his well-being, crying when watching him being punished by a Marleyan officer from the Marley Public Security. Fay's death was what later inspired Grisha to join the Restorationists movement.

Trivia

Fay, Abel Reiss and Ymir Fritz share the same Japanese voice actress.
Fay is a French name, meaning "fairy".
